titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in Argentina ( Braden ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in Argentina ( Braden ) to the Secretary of State

opener.dateline:
Buenos Aires , September 1, 1945—9 p.m.
[Received September 1—8:05 p.m.]

div.p: 2045. Reference Embassy’s telegrams 1441, July 5, 9 p.m. and 1498, July 11, 6 p.m. In absence of any information from Department, I assume it was impossible to raise at PotsdamThe Conference of Berlin between the Heads of Governments of the United States, the United Kingdom, and the Soviet Union and their advisers, July 17–August 2, 1945; the Argentine problem was not taken up at the Conference. question of British attitude toward Argentine question. If this is case, strongly recommend that this be discussed at forthcoming London meeting of Foreign Ministers.For documentation on the First Session of the Council of Foreign Ministers, London, September 11–October 2, 1945, see vol. ii, pp. 99 ff.

While possible application of broad economic sanctions against Argentina is not recommended presently, if Argentina persists in protecting Axis interests and influences the question might arise subsequently, hence discussion thereof with the British now might be desirable in order to pave way for possible later action if needed.
